mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces discount rates and supports equity multiples, so a month-over-month decline in policy rates predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS declines are followed by negative 20d SPX returns in more than 55% of triggered months, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays at or below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above a restrictive threshold, tightening pressure dominates risk assets and higher rates compress valuation multiples, so equities underperform over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% are not associated with negative 20d SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if conditional mean returns are not below the unconditional mean by at least 0.25 standard deviations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in UNRATE signals weakening household income and slower demand, which depresses earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE increases above the trigger and SPX still posts positive 20d returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the signal's 2-year rolling Rank IC is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth and unemployment together identify labor market momentum: rising PAYEMS with stable or falling UNRATE implies stronger near-term consumption and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of higher PAYEMS and non-rising UNRATE does not beat either series alone in predictive power, or if 5d forward returns are not positive in the majority of top-quintile signal dates.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales accelerate, household spending is improving and revenue expectations for large-cap equities rise, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS growth above 0.5% does not precede positive 5d SPX returns more often than not, or if the conditional average return is not above zero by at least 10 bps.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production and housing starts together indicate broad real-activity acceleration, which improves earnings breadth and pushes S&P 500 returns higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined INDPRO-HOUST signal does not outperform single-proxy versions, or if triggered observations fail to produce positive 20d returns in at least 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A negative GDP growth surprise signals weakening aggregate demand and lower expected corporate cash flows, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if negative GDP growth episodes do not coincide with negative 60d SPX returns in the majority of cases, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is elevated while activity weakens, real demand is being squeezed and the equity risk premium should widen, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation > 0.3% m/m and INDPRO declines m/m
failure_condition: Reject if the high-inflation plus falling-output condition does not lead to negative 20d SPX returns at a frequency above 55%, or if the interaction term is not significant in walk-forward tests.
